Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to act as an expert AI in process mining My task is to take a raw Windows system log timestamp action app window and convert it into a structured process mining event log

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data System Log Lets break down the log line by line to understand the users behavior

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This sequence Focus Type Save looks like a complete unit of work on Document1 docx Lets tentatively call this Case 1 Drafting Document1

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a distinct unit of work Handling an email regarding the Annual Meeting Lets call this Case 2 Handling Annual Meeting Email

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Reviewing a PDF This is another unit of work Case 3 Reviewing Report Draft

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Updating a budget Case 4 Updating Budget

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Critical Observation The user returns to Document1 docx This links back to the earlier work on Document1 Is this a new case or the same case

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternative View Maybe the Case is the users work session No thats too broad Better View The Case is the Object Document Email File

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Decision I will define the Case ID based on the specific object being worked on Window title File This provides the most granular and logical process instance view

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For the email I will infer the Case ID as Email Handling Annual Meeting or stick to the object Email Inbox but the former is better analysis

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Refine Activity Naming The prompt asks to translate low level actions FOCUS TYPING into higher level steps

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Raw Focus to High Level Open Start Session or just Open Document Raw Typing to High Level Edit Document Draft Content Raw Save to High Level Save Changes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Switch is tricky In object centric process mining the Switch is the handover In a standard event log one table Switch events usually belong to the source or are just transitions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Strategy Since I need a single table event log I should map the SWITCH event to the case it leaves or the case it enters or omit it if the FOCUS event covers the entry

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets trace Document1 docx Open Focus Type Type Save User switches away Focus Type Save Close This looks like a coherent flow for Document1 docx

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will treat the SWITCH event as an entry point for the To attributes the new Case I will create an activity Open App or View File for the destination case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Constructing the Table Lets iterate through the logs and assign Case IDs and Activity Names

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will structure the output as a table for clarity Columns Case ID Activity Timestamp Resource Additional Info optional derived from keys

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Case Identification Logic Grouping by file document name works best here For the email I synthesized a specific Case ID Email Annual Meeting based on the action metadata because Email Inbox is too generic for a meaningful process instance

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The Case ID Quarterly Report docx tracks the lifespan of the interaction with that specific file The first event is Open Document the second is Resume Editing This correctly captures that the user had it open but was not working on it for a while

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If I present the log sorted by timestamp as is standard the reader will see jumps between cases Case A to Case B to Case C to Case A If I sort by Case ID I lose the global temporal context

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will present the table sorted by Timestamp to reflect the actual system log flow but clearly labeled with Case IDs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Everything seems consistent It captures the flow the interruptions the specific objects and transforms low level logs into business relevant steps
